original_hypothesis.title: Yield Account Rescue Allows Admin to Drain User Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Centralization risk allows a compromised or malicious pool admin to execute arbitrary calls from any user's YieldAccount, draining all staked assets.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: YieldRegistry.rescue allows the POOL_ADMIN to execute arbitrary calls (target.functionCall(data)) from any user's YieldAccount clone. This mechanism bypasses all user ownership checks and can be used to transfer staked assets (stETH, eETH, sDAI) out of every user's account.
preconditions: Attacker compromises the POOL_ADMIN private key or admin acts maliciously

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin constructs calldata to call transfer() on the external yield token (e.g., stETH) from the user's YieldAccount to the admin's address.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin steals 100% of all users' staked yield assets.

economic_questions: Is the POOL_ADMIN a multisig or a single EOA?
Are there any timelocks on the rescue function?
validation_requirements: Verify POOL_ADMIN can call rescue to transfer tokens
Check deployment scripts for admin configuration

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Total Value Locked (TVL) in yield strategies

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete loss of user funds in yield strategies

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
